item.title: v1.0.181
item.description:
• chore: address deferred review feedback (locale date, PID echo, static titles) @leecalcote (#5700)
  • fix(auth): fold + allowlist-check directionless order columns (closes #5691 gap) @leecalcote (#5699)
  • fix(ui): derive error-page type in render to kill the session-expired modal flash @leecalcote (#5698)
  • feat(ui): present 404, 403, and version-incompatibility as distinct error concerns @leecalcote (#5697)
  • fix(ui): render the incompatible-version error instead of a login loop; quiet SPA 404 log @leecalcote (#5695)
  • fix(auth): compare Meshery core version so kanvas v1.0.55-1 passes the min-version gate @leecalcote (#5694)
  • Icon cleanup and import naming compliance @leecalcote (#5546)
  • refactor(auth): guard custom-org-domain resolve behind the org-context check @leecalcote (#5693)
  • fix(auth): silence benign org-domain ERROR on sign-in; clarify linked-account debug logs @leecalcote (#5692)
  • fix(roles): translate camelCase sort column so ?order=roleName works (fixes roles 500) @leecalcote (#5691)

👨🏽‍💻 Contributors 🔗

Thank you to our contributors for making this release possible: @fitzergerald, @leecalcote and @ritzorama

item.title: v1.0.101
item.description:
Hotfix - custom-domain orgs with anchored-fallback credentials hit 1722 at callback 🔗

Custom-domain orgs without their own BYOC IdP credentials (e.g. exoscale.layer5.io) ran into meshery-cloud-1722 \state validation failed\ immediately after GitHub redirected back to /oidc/callback/github. The W2 state binding stamped the request host (exoscale.layer5.io), but the callback legitimately lands at the canonical host (cloud.layer5.io) because the fallback chain anchors redirect_uri there. The validator’s strict host-binding equality refused the cross-host bounce.

item.title: v1.0.98
item.description:
Hotfix - production sign-in restored on cloud.layer5.io (v1.0.97 regression) 🔗

Fixes a regression introduced in v1.0.97 where sign-in via GitHub / Google on cloud.layer5.io failed with meshery-cloud-1729 \direct OIDC sign-in without a Hydra login_challenge is not yet supported\ (Cloudflare shows this as 502 from some POPs). The legacy Kratos selfservice OIDC flow initiated the Hydra login challenge internally; W2’s /oidc/start had no equivalent, so the React /login page’s \Sign in with…\ button forwarded to W2 without a login_challenge and OIDCCallback fell into its fail-closed branch.

item.title: v1.0.97
item.description:
Hotfix - production sign-in restored on cloud.layer5.io 🔗

Fixes a regression where clicking Sign in with GitHub / Sign in with Google on cloud.layer5.io/login redirected users to the upstream OAuth provider with a redirect_uri of https://cloud.layer5.io/kratos/self-service/methods/oidc/callback/{provider}, which GitHub / Google rejected as edirect_uri is not associated with this application\ after the production OAuth App callback URL was updated to the W2 native path /oidc/callback/{provider}.

What changed 🔗

  • OrgAuthData exposes a new UseCloudNativeOidc boolean that is true for both registered custom-domain hosts AND for the canonical Provider Org host (SERVER_BASE_URL’s host). The React login / registration pages now gate the W2 cloud-native redirect on this field, so canonical-host sign-in no longer falls through to the legacy Kratos OIDC flow.
  • A new resolveOIDCOrgID(req) helper in the server handlers prefers the middleware-resolved org and falls back to ProviderOrgID when the request lands on the canonical host. Both OIDCStart and OIDCCallback consume the helper so the state binding’s orgId is symmetric across the round trip on the canonical host.

Rollout notes 🔗

The Layer5 production GitHub OAuth App’s Authorization callback URL is already https://cloud.layer5.io/oidc/callback/github and this deploy restores canonical-host sign-in without further OAuth App changes.

  • fix(catalog): catalog card author avatar blank — useGetUserProfileByIdQuery result accessed as avatar_url; corrected to avatarUrl @jamieplu (#5115)
  • fix(navbar): navbar avatar image missing — useGetUserQuery result now read as avatarUrl @jamieplu (#5115)
  • fix(active-users): active user avatars and list keys broken — userId/avatarUrl aligned to v1beta2.User wire format @jamieplu (#5115)
  • fix(user-management): view-user modal read user_id/avatar_url from useGetUserProfileByIdQuery response; corrected to userId/avatarUrl @jamieplu (#5115)
  • fix(account/profile): account profile page read avatar_url/user_id from useGetUserQuery response and displayed blank username field; corrected to avatarUrl/userId @jamieplu (#5115)
  • fix(user-profile): skip predicate on useGetUserProfileByIdQuery used profile?.userId but query arg is profile?.id; email-signup users (no userId) had their profile detail fetch incorrectly skipped @jamieplu (#5115)
